title: ECONOMY ix. In the Pahlavi Period
units: narrative with cited statistical figures (GNP growth, sector shares, income distribution, etc.)
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica -- academic reference, cite author/article per their citation guidance
notes: Article by M. Hashem Pesaran (Vol. VIII, Fasc. 2, pp. 143-156; published Dec 15, 1997; last updated Jan 16, 2014). Comprehensive scholarly economic history covering Reza Shah's rise (1921) through Mohammad Reza Shah's fall (1979): Trans-Iranian Railway, 1933 oil concession, 1930 foreign exchange crisis and trade monopoly law, WWII disruption, oil nationalization (1951-53) and Mosaddeq coup, the two Seven-Year Plans, White Revolution/land reform (1963), and the 1963-78 industrialization boom with detailed GDP/sector-share/income-distribution statistics. Full bibliography of underlying academic sources (Bharier, Karshenas, Amuzegar, Issawi, Katouzian, etc.) preserved. cia.gov-style bot gating (Cloudflare challenge) blocked plain curl/WebFetch (HTTP 403 with cf-mitigated:challenge header) -- retrieved via the agent's browser tool, which passed the Cloudflare check, then extracted full article text via get_page_text. Saved as clean UTF-8 plain text (not a scrape of raw HTML) since no PDF/print version was offered by the site; content and structure verified against the rendered page.
